gaps: whole_plant=1: all four shots are either mass-drift or flower close-ups; no clear single-plant habit showing the wiry-stemmed, 1–2 ft bushy form. A shot of one full plant isolated or at the garden edge would lift this to 2.

whole_plant=1: su-plant shot is a single young plant shot from above at an angle — the flower is cropped at the top of the thumb and the plant base is cut off; shows ferny foliage well but not full upright habit. Need a clear side-on view of a mature plant showing full stem-to-tip height.

thumb_framing=1: su-plant-t.jpg shows the plant shot from above, flower mostly at top edge of frame, base cut off; framing conveys the feathery foliage but the whole-plant feature is off-centre and partially clipped.
seasonal=1: all 7 shots are summer-only; larkspur has a distinct rosette-foliage fall phase (fall-sown) and spring emergence worth showing — no CC spring/fall shots sourced yet
whole_plant=1: su-habit shows a single flowering stem mid-stand (cluttered) rather than a clear single-plant form; no shot of an isolated plant showing height, branching, and palmate leaves together
foliage=1: palmate compound leaves visible in su-habit and fa-pods but always secondary/blurred behind flowers or pods; no dedicated crisp leaf close-up
whole_plant=1: su-habit shows plants against a wall/pavement corner — cluttered background, base partially cropped; need a clean garden-bed habit shot showing the full upright spike form
seed_pods=1: su-pods shot has pods visible but they are small secondary elements alongside flowers; a dedicated close-up of the distinctive rounded 2-chambered capsules would score 2

thumb_framing=1: su-habit thumbnail frames the plant in the lower corner against bare pavement with one spike cut off at the top; su-foliage thumbnail shows the plant small with a distracting diagonal shadow; su-pods has the pods quite small in a complex scene
seasonal: only summer+fall; winter interest noted in plant record but no winter or spring shot

seasonal=1: five shots are all spring; plant is described as 'reliably evergreen dark green mat year-round' with winter=true and notable purplish winter tints. A fall or winter mat shot (showing the evergreen foliage out of bloom) is the missing season. Gap is fillable from iNat (search V. liwanensis, phenology=no_evidence or fall months).

whole_plant=1: su-structure shot shows the mat blooming in context but is a tight mid-distance shot — never shows the mat edge-on with clear spread against soil or a wall/slope to convey scale and groundcover form.

bloom_in_context=1: has close-up (su-close) and a bloom-covered mat (su-structure), which technically provides both elements, but su-structure is a mid-range shot that blurs the boundary between single-flower close-up and whole-mat shot; the mat edge and surroundings are not visible, so context reads as 'patch of flowers' rather than 'mat spreading across a rock garden'. Upgrade su-structure to a wider shot showing the mat on its substrate, or add a true habitat/context shot.

thumb_framing=1: su-habit-t.jpg — plant is small and distant in a wide habitat shot; the mat itself is identifiable but pushed to lower-left with a large boulder dominating the frame; feature is not lost but not well-centred

seasonal=1: all 3 shots are spring (bloom period); the silver-gray woolly mat persists through summer, fall, and winter — a summer or fall/winter foliage shot would show the plant's year-round presence and mat character outside bloom

foliage=1: all shots show foliage only as background or secondary context; no dedicated shot where lance-linear leaf shape and hairy texture are the clear subject — the habit shot has visible foliage but the flowers dominate

whole_plant=1: su-habit shows 3 flowers at knee-height with cluttered habitat background; the basal rosette and overall plant form are not clean. Needs a cleaner whole-plant shot showing the mounding clump clearly.

bloom_in_context=1: su-bloom (the 'drift/mass' shot) shows only 2 individual plants in tall grass, not a real drift — insufficient for the 'mass of flowers' criterion. The full image confirms this. Needs a true colony/drift shot.
thumb_framing=1: su-foliage thumbnail crops to a tiny isolated rosette (mostly bare soil/mulch with the plant small in the lower-centre); feature is legible but off-centre and small.
seed_pods: cranesbill seed beaks are a distinctive ID feature — no shot captured
seasonal: summer+fall only; spring is peak bloom season and is unrepresented
foliage: su-foliage thumb shows a face-on flower with needle leaves visible only as small side elements; no shot where the fine needle-like foliage is the primary legible subject
seed_pods: su-seedpods thumb has a large hand prominent in foreground — pods are shown but framing is distracting (score 1)
thumb_framing: su-seedpods hand intrusion drops the set below 2; su-foliage framed as a flower shot rather than a foliage shot

seasonal: all shots are summer; plant blooms May–Jul and has spring/fall interest (seed capsules in late summer already shown); a spring shot when the plant first emerges and opens its first flowers would fill the gap
whole_plant=0: all three shots are close-in on the plant; none shows the full mat width, mound shape, or how it sits in a border/wall. No clear view of the complete plant form.

bloom_in_context=1: sp-bloom shows a dense mat smothered in flowers (mass context good) but there is no single close-up flower shot that reads as the 'close-up' half of the pair — sp-flower is a mid-distance clump edge shot, not a macro of individual corymbs. The sp-bloom shot does the context work well; what's missing is a crisp tight macro of a single corymb cluster.

seasonal=1: all three shots are spring (bloom). The plant is described as evergreen with glossy dark foliage year-round — a winter or late-fall foliage shot would demonstrate that distinctive character. The sp-foliage shot shows leaves, but in spring alongside white flowers, not in its winter/dormant form.

foliage=1: the characteristic ferny blue-green foliage appears in sp-habit-t.jpg and sp-bloom2-t.jpg but always as secondary context behind flowers; no shot where leaves are the clear subject with readable shape and texture
seasonal=1: spring and summer covered; no fall/winter shot (plant goes dormant, but fall seedhead or yellowing foliage would add value)

whole_plant=1: su-mass shows a multi-stemmed plant in dry field context (closest to habit), but cuts off at the base and mixes with surrounding vegetation; su-habit full-image shows a single stem from ground to blossom but the thumb crops to stems only with no visible flower
foliage=1: su-foliage shows thread-fine narrow leaves but the image is soft/slightly blurred and the leaves are secondary to stems; leaf shape marginally readable but not crisp
thumb_framing=1: su-habit thumbnail is badly cropped — shows only bare stems and buds with the flower cut from frame; su-foliage thumbnail is noticeably blurry
seasonal=1: all 4 shots are summer; missing spring rosette/foliage and a fall/late-season shot (seed heads attract goldfinches — notable fall feature)

foliage=1: sp-foliage shows bract-leaves around the cyathia but true oblong vegetative leaves are not the clear subject; the shot reads more as a second flower close-up than a foliage study — need a shot where the ovate, alternate stem leaves (not bracts) and their waxy texture are clearly readable

whole_plant=1: su-habit shows good leaf detail but the top of the plant is cut off and there is no clear single-plant habit shot. su-bloom is a whole-garden field, not one plant's form. A mid-distance shot of one upright branching dahlia plant showing its full height would complete this.

seasonal=1: all 3 shots are summer. Dahlia blooms July through first hard frost (into October); a fall shot showing the plant at peak late-season bloom or the foliage before frost is absent. Single season only.
foliage: su-foliage slot is occupied by a flower shot (mislabeled); the silver-blue evergreen mat is only seen as background in sp-habit — no clean foliage-only shot
thumb_framing: sp-flower is underexposed/dark; su-foliage slot shows a flower not foliage
seasonal: spring+summer only; plant is noted as winter:true (evergreen mat persists) but no winter shot

whole_plant: su-habit thumbnail is cluttered with competing plants, litter, and grass; the sprawling groundcover form is hard to read — needs a cleaner habit shot showing the low mound with its willow-like leaves

flowers: su-flower2 full is face-on and shows the large lemon cup well; BUT the thumbnail crops almost entirely to hand and narrow leaf bases with only the petal edge visible (thumb_framing=0 for that shot); su-bloom shows two damaged/insect-nibbled flowers from an awkward low angle — neither shot delivers a clean face-on cup in the thumbnail
front_facing: both flower shots have framing/orientation issues in the thumb (hand obscures su-flower2; su-bloom is oblique and partially damaged)
bloom_in_context: only two flowers in su-bloom — no true drift or massed-bloom shot; the plant blooms prolifically and a wider drift context shot is missing

seed_pods: fa-pods shows the four-winged capsules but they appear small, early-stage (still greenish-red), and lie flat against rocky soil — a shot of the distinctive fully-developed papery winged capsules filling the frame is needed
thumb_framing: su-flower2 thumb is almost entirely a hand — worst-framed thumb in the audit (score 0 for that specific shot, pulls set to 1)

front_facing=1: su-flower (dense spike close-up) shows the pea-flowers in tight profile along the spike; no shot captures individual pea-flowers truly face-on (showing the banner petal clearly front-on from below the spike). The spike close-up is the practical way to show lupine flowers but the individual florets are mostly shown from the side. A low-angle shot looking up into an open floret would lift this.

seasonal=1: all four shots are summer; plant is a perennial with bold palmate foliage from early spring and can look tatty by late summer. A spring emerging-foliage shot would document the early-season form. Gap is fillable from iNat (budding phenology, spring months).
foliage=1: su-foliage-t.jpg shows leaves along a stem but the frame is busy with background foliage and a tiny flower at the edge; leaf shape is readable but the shot is not a clean foliage study
thumb_framing=1: su-habit2-t.jpg crops into an incomplete view of the panicle and adjacent plant; su-bloom-t.jpg shows an overhead massed planting where no single plant form is clear
seasonal=1: all 6 shots are summer only; no spring emerging growth or fall/senescence view

foliage=1: su-foliage shot is dominated by a bumblebee on a flower; the deeply-lobed palmate foliage is visible in the background but is secondary/blurred — need a shot where the lobed leaf shape and texture are the clear subject rather than the flower
seasonal=1: all 4 shots are summer; the plant is known for red-orange fall foliage color but no fall shot exists — add a fall color shot to score 2

seed_pods=1: the 3-lobed capsules (seed pods) of euphorbia are visible incidentally in sp-bloom and fa-detail but there is no shot dedicated to showing the capsules clearly as a feature. Not a critical gap — the capsules are not the main ornamental feature — but a close-up in the late-summer/fall seed-set window would fully cover this axis.
seed_pods: fluffy anemone seedheads are a notable late-season feature — none captured
seasonal: summer+fall only; spring emergence (bold foliage flush) not represented

foliage: su-foliage thumb shows a hand holding a leaf stem in front of a wooden fence with mixed background plants — the pinnate leaf is visible but the hand and cluttered background make it score 1; a clean in-situ shot of the basal rosette/pinnate leaves is needed
thumb_framing: su-habit has the nearest pincushion head slightly out of focus in the foreground with the rest of the plant behind; su-foliage hand dominates (score 1 for the set)
seasonal: all shots are summer; plant blooms Jul–Sep with basal rosette persisting into late fall — a fall shot of the dying-back rosette or late flowers would round out the reel

seasonal=1: all four shots are spring; plant reblooms into summer and fall with deadheading and is fully perennial. A summer or fall rebloom shot would show the extended season. Gap is fillable from iNat (summer/fall months, flowering phenology).

flowers=1: sp-flower (the dedicated close-up) is shot looking down from above into a half-open bowl — the petals' full crepe-paper texture, the fringed edge, and the stamens are visible but the flower is not fully open face-on. sp-bloom2 is the better close-up (multiple fully-open blooms face-on, fringed petals clear), but it is labeled as the bloom-context shot. The reel lacks a single clean, fully-open, face-on close-up.

front_facing=1: sp-flower is shot from directly above/slightly angled into a half-open bloom — not truly face-on. sp-bloom2 has two face-on blooms but they compete with each other and the framing is wide. sp-bloom (chefao shot) is overhead looking into one bloom with the basal leaves visible — again not a clean face-on front. The reel has no single definitive face-on flower portrait.

thumb_framing=1: sp-flower-t.jpg crops the bloom from the bottom — only the upper half of the flower and the distinctively ruffled petals are visible; the full bowl shape and stamens are cut off in the thumbnail. sp-bloom-t.jpg shows the flower viewed from above, off-centre at right.

seasonal=1: all six shots are spring/early-summer bloom or the summer seed pod. The plant has a well-known autumn foliage re-emergence (basal rosette returns in September) — a fall shot of that re-emergent foliage would complete the lifecycle story and help gardeners recognize it in autumn. This is achievable with iNat phenology filtering.
seed_pods=1: fa-bloom-t.jpg shows some browning/spent heads incidentally but no dedicated shot of the feathery persistent seedheads that are a noted feature; a cleaner seedhead shot would score 2
seasonal=1: all 4 shots are fall only; no summer foliage/stem shot or spring emergence — the plant is tall and leafy all season and a summer stem shot would be useful
whole_plant=1: sp-habit shows closed buds at low angle against litter — flowers are closed, clump form partially obscured; no shot of the full open clump in bloom from a normal viewing angle
foliage=1: sp-foliage shows pre-bloom silky buds on hairy stems, not the characteristic ferny leaf rosette that emerges post-bloom; the actual foliage (dissected, hairy leaves) is not represented

bloom_in_context=1: sp-flower is a tight cluster of open cups that reads as both close-up and mini-mass, but there is no standing-back shot of the whole clump in full open bloom showing low prairie mounding habit

thumb_framing=1: sp-habit thumbnail is a low-angle close shot of closed buds against litter — subject small and habitat cluttered; sp-foliage thumbnail shows buds in lower-left with wasted sky/background space above

seasonal=1: only spring shots (bloom and seedhead); the plant has attractive ferny summer foliage and the blurb notes winter interest (winter:true) — missing summer foliage and ideally a winter clump shot

whole_plant=1: su-habit frames the plant from the right side only (base cut off below frame, top cut off); a dead twig intrudes top-left; the plant's characteristic arching multi-stem form is not clearly read — need a shot showing the full plant base-to-crown

foliage=1: su-foliage is dark, low-contrast, and shows leaves as secondary backdrop to a seedhead; the glaucous blue-gray leaf color, ovate shape and smooth texture are not clearly readable — need a cleaner, brighter foliage close-up
thumb_framing=1: su-habit thumbnail is dominated by grey water background with the plant cropped to the right half; su-foliage thumbnail is dark and the leaves are small and unreadable at card size

front_facing=1: su-flower shows dense spikes photographed from an angled/side perspective; individual florets are visible but the shot is more of a mass-of-spikes angle than a crisp face-on of a single floret. For spike-type salvias a side view of the spike is standard, but a tighter shot isolating individual tubular florets would score 2.

thumb_framing=1: su-habit thumbnail is a portrait-orientation image cropped to 3:2, cutting the clear context (the full image is outstanding but the crop reduces framing quality, leaving the plant noticeably smaller in the thumb). fa-foliage thumbnail shows the plant amid dense weedy growth with limited isolation.

seasonal=1: only summer (2 shots) and fall (1 foliage shot). Missing spring early-growth or a second-flush rebloom shot in late summer/fall when the plant is in its August rebloom. Three shots total is thin for a plant with a documented rebloom habit and aromatic fall foliage interest.
whole_plant: su-bloom thumbnail cuts the plant top off the frame; mostly shows meadow background — habit of the compact clump on wiry stems never clearly shown
foliage: su-foliage slot is actually a flower shot (mislabeled); the characteristic ferny gray-green basal foliage is never shown
bloom_in_context: su-bloom is the intended mass shot but the plant is cropped out of frame — need a shot showing the clump covered in floating pincushion blooms
thumb_framing: su-bloom thumb has the flower cut at top and plant missing from frame
seasonal: summer+fall only; late-spring bloom onset unrepresented

seed_pods: fa-pods thumbnail shows the spent head from behind/above while the bloom is still partially red — the shot reads more as a late flower than a true dried seedhead; a shot of fully-dried brown calyces on tall upright winter stems would score 2

seasonal=1: three shots all summer; plant is a perennial with a spring basal-rosette emergence stage and can be found in bloom from June through early fall. A spring emerging-basal-rosette shot, or a late-fall/early-spring rosette shot, would complete the seasonal picture. Gap is fillable from iNat (spring months or fall months, budding/no_evidence phenology).

foliage=1: su-foliage shot shows the palmate leaves with rain droplets — leaves are recognizable but the shot is soft/slightly out of focus across the frame (the background grass is equally sharp); the defining 'silvery' texture of the hairs is not legible. The silkiness is this plant's key ID feature. Need a shot in direct sunlight where the silver sheen is clearly visible.

seed_pods=1: fa-pods shows dried calyces and remnant petals on a bare stem — the image is labeled as a seed spike with ripening pods, but actual ripe seed pods (the hairy legume-type pods that split explosively) are not visible; what's shown are old empty flower calyces. A shot of the actual hairy pea-pod seed pods, ideally fat and pre-split, is missing.

thumb_framing=1: su-foliage-t.jpg is a soft low-contrast shot — the silvery leaf feature is not crisp in the thumb; su-flower-t.jpg has the flower spike at upper-right with the leaves largely cut off at the frame edges; fa-pods-t.jpg shows a distant bare stem against blurred grass with almost no readable content at card scale.
foliage=1: leaf shape is visible in su-context-t.jpg and su-habit-t.jpg but always secondary to the spikes; no shot focused on the basal rosette or stem leaves showing texture clearly

seasonal=1: all 6 shots are summer; no spring emerging rosette or fall/senescence view; the blurb notes 'rosette of dark green leaves from spring' and 'tidy through fall' — those phases have no coverage

thumb_framing=1: su-flower thumbnail shows clusters at a distance against rocky soil — individual florets small and partially obscured; su-foliage has the flower cluster off left-center with foliage taking the right side; neither is a tight, clearly-centered feature shot

note: shots depict the species (Glandularia canadensis, rose-pink form) rather than the named 'Homestead Purple' cultivar (deep violet-purple); the habit and flower structure convey the plant type well but the color differs from what the plant record describes

seed_pods=1: su-pods shows the slender silique pods on upper stems but the shot is somewhat soft/blurry and the pods are small relative to the frame — a crisper close-up showing 3-5 siliques in sharp detail would score 2

thumb_framing=1: sp-bloom thumbnail places the whole plant as a tiny element in the lower center of a large expanse of white gravel — the plant is identifiable but small, not 'centered and clearly visible'; su-pods thumbnail is blurry and the pods are difficult to read at card scale

foliage=1: fa-foliage shows the wiry branching stem with the heath-like needle leaves and flowers opening, but the leaves are secondary to the flowers in the frame and the fine texture is not the primary readable element. No dedicated shot of the distinctive fine heath-like foliage in summer before bloom, which is the key identifying feature.

thumb_framing=1: wi-seeds thumbnail is noticeably out-of-focus (shallow DOF with camera aimed between the seedhead clusters) — the feathery tufts are soft and hard to read at card scale. The full image is similarly blurry.

seasonal=1: 5 of 6 shots are fall (bloom window) plus 1 winter seedhead. The plant spends summer as a wiry, distinctive foliage mound — no summer shot. Spring emergence also absent. Missing the summer vegetative state.
front_facing: fa-close shows a cluster of daisy flowers at varied angles; no single clean face-on flower
seed_pods: fa-seedheads shows the pappus seedhead but it is small in the upper-left corner against mostly brown prairie straw — feature poorly framed
thumb_framing: fa-seedheads thumb has the seedhead feature small and off-center in a cluttered dried-grass frame
seasonal: only fall shots; spring/summer foliage growth unrepresented (all-season foliage plant)

seasonal: summer + fall covered but no winter shot; care notes state 'dried brown seedheads stand attractively through fall and winter, feeding goldfinches and sparrows' — a winter seedhead shot on bare stems would close the gap and match the plant's advertised four-season interest

whole_plant=1: su-close is named 'close' but the thumbnail crop renders as a cluttered tangle of stems and heads; the full image (portrait) is better but the card thumb doesn't read as a clear whole-plant habit. A landscape-oriented shot of the full mound (silvery foliage + golden heads as a clear dome) would score 2.

thumb_framing=1: su-close-t.jpg crop is a busy mid-field tangle of stems — the 720×480 smart-crop doesn't isolate a clear subject; it reads as background texture rather than a legible habit shot. The su-structure and su-foliage thumbs are well-centered.

seasonal=1: all three shots are summer; plant is described as 'silver-grey foliage from spring through fall; seedheads add minor winter texture' and foliage is semi-evergreen in mild winters. A spring foliage shot or a fall/early-winter seedhead shot would document the off-bloom season. Gap is fillable from iNat or Commons (spring foliage, fall seedheads).
seasonal=1: spring and fall covered; winter interest (bronze-purple leaf colour) is a named feature and listed in 'seasons' but has no shot; a winter foliage shot is the clear gap

whole_plant=1: su-habit is a shot looking up into the shrub canopy from below/inside — shows massed bloom beautifully but gives no sense of the 6–9 ft vase-shaped shrub form; no standing-back full-shrub silhouette

foliage=1: all 3 shots are in-bloom with leaves always secondary to white flowers; oval opposite leaves are glimpsed in su-foliage and su-flower but never shown as the primary subject — leaf shape and texture readable only if you look for it

thumb_framing=1: su-habit thumbnail is a crowded within-canopy view with no clear shrub outline or sense of scale; the subject is blooms (good) but the composition is too internal to read as a whole-plant habit shot
seasonal=1: all 3 shots are summer bloom; the card notes 'good yellow fall color' and 'arching vase-shaped stems provide winter structure' — both are unrepresented seasons for a multi-season shrub

thumb_framing=1: wi-framework thumbnail shows a dense, cluttered thicket interior — the stems merge with background and the plant's bold multi-stem structure is not clearly legible at card scale. The full image is also dark and cluttered. This winter shot would benefit from replacement with a cleaner, more isolated winter silhouette.

seasonal=1: summer (3), fall (1), winter (1) — no spring shot. Spring leaf-out, especially the dramatic burgundy new foliage on purple-leaf cultivars like 'Royal Purple', is a notable feature. Not critical but the season gap is present.
foliage: no dedicated foliage shot; leaves visible in the whole-shrub shot but leaf shape/texture not readable as the primary subject
flowers: su-flower has a large hand/finger prominently obscuring the frame — paired yellow tubular flowers visible but partially blocked and not a clean shot
front_facing: flowers in su-flower viewed from below/side around hand; not face-on

bloom_in_context: no clear mass-in-bloom shot; su-habit shows shrub with small distant orange bracts rather than yellow flowers in peak bloom — the defining yellow-flower+red-bract display is not shown at scale
seed_pods: su-pods shows the distinctive twin berries in red bracts but berries appear shriveled/spent rather than crisp glossy-black; image is low-resolution with clutter background
thumb_framing: su-flower has hand dominating frame; su-habit shows landscape context with sky gap; su-foliage shows whole shrub without close leaf detail; overall framing weak across reel
seasonal: only summer shots; yellow fall foliage and winter bare architectural stems unrepresented

front_facing=1: lavender spikes present their flowers in a spiral/whorl along a vertical spike, not face-on in the daisy sense. The su-flower shot shows the spike from a natural three-quarter angle with a bumblebee, which is the best practical view of lavender flowers. Fully face-on individual lavender florets are rarely the subject of iNat photos; this may be a structural limit of the plant. Capped at 1 unless a shot can be found looking directly into a whorl of open florets.

foliage=1: the silvery aromatic leaf is the defining non-bloom character of this plant, but no shot isolates it — all four shots focus on the flowering mound. In su-habit and su-bloom the leaves are visible but secondary to the flower spikes; su-flower shows the leaf but at low resolution (low-quality cell phone shot with heavy noise). A clean shot showing the thick, silver-felted leaf up close is missing.

seasonal=1: all four shots are summer. The plant is semi-evergreen and the blurb/seasons field specifically calls out winter structure and the violet + mauve bract combo extending Jun–Nov. A fall or winter shot showing the persistent mauve bracts and silvery winter form would lift this to excellent. This is achievable via iNat (phenology=budding/no-evidence or late-fall date filter).
whole_plant=1: su-habit-t.jpg and su-bloom-t.jpg show the plant from above/overhead; the mound form is visible but the shots are top-down angle rather than at-grade showing height and spread clearly

flowers=1: the tubular flowers (su-flower-t.jpg) are shown at mid-range on the full plant; no close-up of a single or tight cluster of flowers showing petal detail and colour clearly — all shots are too far for individual flower detail
seasonal=1: all 4 shots are summer; the plant is evergreen with winter interest (noted in plant data) but no winter foliage shot exists

foliage=1: su-flowers shows aromatic cut foliage alongside flowers but leaves are narrow and secondary; su-flowers2 (macro of flower spike) shows hairy calyx more than leaf form; no shot where the silver dissected foliage is the clear primary subject

front_facing=1: su-flowers2 is the closest — a spike macro with a few tubular flowers partially face-on — but the labiate flowers on their spike are naturally side-facing; no shot captures multiple flowers clearly face-on in the way a daisy or poppy shot can; capped: the flower form (small tubular on a spike) makes full front_facing=2 very difficult with available CC photos
seasonal=1: summer and fall covered; the card records winter:true (silver stems persist and provide structural interest through winter) — missing a winter stems shot

flowers=1: sp-flowers is backlit and angled, making the individual small white flowers hard to read; sp-flowers2 is better but still a mid-distance shot of a raceme cluster rather than a crisp close-up of the flower detail — neither shot gives a clear look at the individual 5-petaled flowers

front_facing=1: both flower shots show the racemes from a slight angle or distance; no shot gives a face-on close-up view of the small white flowers — add a crisp macro of an individual raceme or several flowers face-on to score 2
foliage: rose compound leaves visible in summer shots but always secondary to flowers — no dedicated foliage shot showing leaflet shape and texture
seasonal: summer+fall only; spring bud-break and winter bare-cane structure (both mentioned in plant record) unrepresented

seasonal: all 4 shots are summer; the vine is semi-deciduous and blooms June–October; no fall shot showing the vine structure late in the season or any transition from peak bloom; a fall or late-summer shot would complete the reel

seed_pods=1: fa-seeds-t shows a single isolated fluffy seedhead (close-up of one), and the caption notes it is from Clematis viticella rather than jackmanii specifically; the blurb/seasons mention 'fluffy silver seedheads into fall/winter' as a feature. A shot of the vine bearing multiple silvery seedheads — showing how it looks as a full display — would score 2. The existing shot is partial (single seedhead, possible species mismatch). Fillable from iNat (C. jackmanii or C. ×jackmanii, phenology=fruiting, fall months).

foliage=0: su-foliage shot shows compound rose leaves — but every leaflet has severe black-spot disease damage (large black blotches covering most of the leaf surface). This is a diseased specimen and does not represent healthy rambling-rose foliage. It must be replaced with a clean, healthy-foliage shot.

thumb_framing=1: fa-hips-t.jpg shows what was described as 'orange-red hips' but the thumbnail is a busy, dark, telephoto shot of pink flowers and dark foliage — the hips are barely visible small dots against a cluttered background; the supposed seasonal interest is not legible at card scale. Also su-foliage-t.jpg foregrounds diseased leaves which is a negative quality signal.

seasonal=1: the reel covers summer (flower, habit, foliage) and fall (hips) — two seasons, which is good. However ramblers are notable in winter for their long arching cane framework, and a winter cane shot (especially under snow or frost) would complete the story. The fa-hips shot is also weak (see thumb_framing), so the fall season is under-served.

whole_plant=1: su-habit-t.jpg shows the vine covering a roofline from below/side — the coverage/mass reads well but the base, support structure, and characteristic twining form are not shown clearly; more of a context shot than a habit shot

thumb_framing=1: su-foliage-t.jpg shows a single juvenile leaf whorl close to the ground with dead leaf litter background — feature is clear but the shot reads as a seedling rather than a mature vine; su-bloom-t.jpg has the flower cluster pushed into the left edge half-hidden by a large leaf
